name: reschedule to an EARLIER time should still win (appointment time-binding)
description: SPEC (docs/reference/ANALYSIS.md 'Temporal tokens and appointment identity'): an appointment's scheduled time is a supersedable binding where the NEWEST note's instruction wins regardless of whether the new time is earlier or later — 'mint a new token from the new note, supersede the binding'. Here 'Dr. Reyes Friday' is moved EARLIER to Wednesday. The spec-correct result: the Wednesday time is the active binding and Friday is superseded. GAP (closed): supersession.decide used to compare VALIDITY time (valid_from) for every state, so the earlier Wednesday candidate failed the `key(candidate) >= key(current)` test and landed in the retrospective branch, leaving the stale Friday time active. Schedule-binding predicates (SCHEDULE_PREDICATES: scheduledTime/scheduled_time) now order by reported_at — the latest reschedule instruction wins in either direction — while ordinary states keep validity ordering. Lever B (ENTITY_GRAPH_INGEST_V2 §5): this clean state/relationship supersession now supersedes SILENTLY with retained history — no fact_conflict card is filed.

steps.body: Appointment with Dr. Reyes is Friday the 19th at 2pm.

facts.statement: Appointment with Dr. Reyes scheduled for Friday 2026-06-19 at 2pm.

steps.body: Dr. Reyes called — the appointment got pulled earlier to Wednesday the 17th at 2pm.

facts.statement: Appointment with Dr. Reyes moved earlier to Wednesday 2026-06-17 at 2pm.
